value.reason: The cited page is about "Remote connections" — specifically the Remote Control capability (mobile/web access to a Codex host), which is explicitly listed as an ADJACENT capability that must be distinct from Notifications. While the page does contain one bullet point mentioning "Get notified when Codex completes a task or needs your attention," this appears only as a sub-feature of the Remote Control workflow, not as a standalone Notifications capability. The page's primary subject is setting up and using remote connections (mobile app pairing, QR codes, SSH hosts, host settings, etc.). It does not document a dedicated Push Notifications feature as a shipped, standalone capability. The cell's label ("Task Completion Notifications") and description (framing alerts/push notifications as a distinct, independent capability) are not supported by this page, which is fundamentally about remote control access surfaces — the very adjacent capability the Notifications cell must be distinguished from.
